title: Q2 Quiz 2 Draft: Audio, Titles, Color, and Final Export

questions.text: Which Resolve page is designed for audio editing and mixing?
questions.explanation: Fairlight is the audio-focused page in Resolve.

questions.text: What does solo do on an audio track?
choices: Plays that track while temporarily muting others.
Changes the clip color to blue.
questions.correctAnswer: Plays that track while temporarily muting others.
questions.explanation: Solo is useful for hearing one track clearly while mixing.

questions.text: What does mute do on an audio track?
choices: Turns that track off during playback.
questions.correctAnswer: Turns that track off during playback.
questions.explanation: Mute disables playback from that track until it is turned back on.

questions.text: Why should audio tracks be named or colored?
choices: To keep dialogue, music, ambience, and effects organized.
To make the export render faster every time.
To remove the need for a timeline.
questions.correctAnswer: To keep dialogue, music, ambience, and effects organized.
questions.explanation: Track organization helps editors find and balance layers quickly.

questions.text: What is a title used for in an edit?
choices: Readable text such as a title card, name label, or lower third.
questions.correctAnswer: Readable text such as a title card, name label, or lower third.
questions.explanation: Titles communicate text information inside the video.

questions.text: Which panel is commonly used to adjust title text, position, and selected effect settings?
questions.explanation: The Inspector exposes controls for selected clips, titles, transitions, and effects.

choices: A point in time that stores a value so a property can change over time.
A folder inside the Media Pool.
The first day of the grading period.
questions.correctAnswer: A point in time that stores a value so a property can change over time.
questions.explanation: Keyframes make motion or property changes happen over time.

questions.text: What is the Color page used for?
questions.explanation: The Color page is where Resolve handles shot color, contrast, exposure, and grading work.

choices: The strength or intensity of colors.
The length of the render queue.
questions.correctAnswer: The strength or intensity of colors.
questions.explanation: Saturation controls how strong or muted colors appear.

questions.text: Which controls mainly affect dark, middle, and bright parts of an image?
questions.explanation: Lift affects darker tones, gamma affects midtones, and gain affects brighter tones.

questions.text: Which page is used to render or export the finished timeline?
questions.explanation: The Deliver page is used to render the timeline into a finished video file.

choices: A list of export jobs waiting to render.
A list of raw clips not imported.
A set of A/B class periods.
questions.correctAnswer: A list of export jobs waiting to render.
questions.explanation: Resolve adds export jobs to the render queue before rendering.

questions.text: Identify the term: the method used to compress and store video or audio data, such as H.264 or H.265.
questions.explanation: A codec controls how video/audio is encoded and compressed.

questions.text: Identify the term: background room tone or environmental sound that helps a scene feel present.
questions.explanation: Ambience fills the scene with realistic background sound.

questions.text: Name one final quality check an editor should make before exporting.
questions.correctAnswer: A final check could include audio clarity, title readability, color consistency, timeline gaps, file name, or export settings.
questions.explanation: A strong answer names a real pre-export check that affects viewer experience or file management.
